01_initial_request.input_data: Missense variants, protein sequences, and human/primate variant population frequency data
01_initial_request.user_intent: To classify missense variants as pathogenic or benign to close the variant interpretation gap and aid in rare disease diagnosis
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Achieve 90% precision on the ClinVar dataset while providing predictions for all possible human missense variants

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AlphaFold accurately predicts protein structure from sequence. [Gap] The model needs to learn evolutionary constraints and amino acid distributions to predict variant effects, not just structure. [Decision] To achieve a foundation for variant prediction, chose to use AlphaFold (modified) to perform structure pretraining and masked language modeling on protein sequences and MSAs, expecting to obtain pretrained model weights capable of sequence-structure understanding.

parameters.loss_configuration: higher weights on the masked MSA reconstruction loss
parameters.input_data: protein sequences and multiple sequence alignments (MSAs)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Pretrained model achieving structure prediction performance comparable to AlphaFold while learning amino acid distributions.

references.relevance: Foundation model for protein structure prediction used as the base architecture.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Pretrained model captures structure and sequence distribution. [Gap] Model needs to predict pathogenicity specifically, avoiding human curation bias inherent in clinical databases. [Decision] To achieve unbiased pathogenicity classification, chose to use AlphaMissense to perform fine-tuning on human and primate population frequency data (weak labels), expecting to obtain a fine-tuned pathogenicity prediction model.

parameters.benign_labels: variants frequently observed in human and primate populations
parameters.pathogenic_labels: variants absent from human and primate populations
parameters.loss_weights: weights in the loss function that reduce the contribution of rare variants
parameters.sampling_strategy: sample a missense variant from the pathogenic set for each observed variant
parameters.refinement: self-distillation by using preliminary AlphaMissense models to filter out unobserved variants predicted to be likely benign

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fine-tuned model predicting pathogenicity scores without explicitly training on clinical annotations.

references.relevance: Approach for defining benign and pathogenic labels using population frequency data (PrimateAI).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw model scores need to be interpretable probabilities for clinical use. [Gap] Scores need calibration against known clinical labels to set precision thresholds for classification. [Decision] To achieve calibrated probability scores, chose to use Univariate Logistic Regression to perform calibration on ClinVar validation set, expecting to obtain calibrated pathogenicity scores ranging between 0 and 1.

parameters.class_balance: equal number of pathogenic and benign variants per gene

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calibrated AlphaMissense scores interpreted as approximate probability of a variant being clinically pathogenic.

references.relevance: Methodology for calibrating scores and choosing cutoffs for precision.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model performance needs validation on clinical benchmarks to ensure utility. [Gap] Need to quantify accuracy on known pathogenic/benign variants without training leakage. [Decision] To achieve performance benchmarking, chose to use Statistical Analysis to evaluate performance on ClinVar test set, expecting to obtain auROC and precision metrics.

parameters.balancing: balancing the number of pathogenic and benign variants per gene
parameters.metric: area under the receiver operator curve (auROC)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: auROC of 0.940 on ClinVar test variants; cutoffs chosen yielding 90% precision for likely pathogenic and likely benign classes.

references.relevance: ClinVar database used as the gold-standard benchmark for clinical variant classification.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Clinical labels are sparse and biased; experimental data provides orthogonal validation. [Gap] Need to verify agreement with multiplexed assays of variant effect (MAVE) to ensure biological relevance. [Decision] To achieve experimental validation, chose to use Statistical Analysis to compare predictions against ProteinGym and additional MAVE data, expecting to obtain Spearman correlation metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mean Spearman correlation on ProteinGym: 0.514; on additional MAVE benchmark: 0.450.

references.relevance: ProteinGym dataset used for benchmarking variant effect predictors against MAVE data.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Individual variant predictions are useful, but proteome-wide coverage is the goal to close the interpretation gap. [Gap] Need to generate predictions for all possible human missense variants to provide a community resource. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive coverage, chose to use AlphaMissense to perform inference on all canonical human proteins, expecting to obtain a database of missense variant predictions.

parameters.substitutions: 216 million possible single amino acid substitutions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Database of predictions for all possible human missense variants; 32% classified as likely pathogenic and 57% as likely benign.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Gene-level constraint is hard to measure for short genes using population data alone (LOEUF). [Gap] Need to assess if model predicts cell essentiality, especially for LOEUF-underpowered genes. [Decision] To achieve gene-level functional significance quantification, chose to use Statistical Analysis to compare gene-level scores against DepMap cell essentiality data, expecting to obtain auROC metrics for essentiality prediction.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: auROC of 0.88 for cell essentiality in LOEUF underpowered genes, outperforming LOEUF (0.81).

references.relevance: DepMap data used for experimentally determined cell essentiality.
LOEUF metric used for comparison of gene constraint.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Benchmarking against ClinVar clinical annotations, Multiplexed Assays of Variant Effect (MAVE), Deciphering Developmental Disorders (DDD) cohort, and Cell Essentiality data (DepMap).

clinvar_auroc.interpretation: State-of-the-art performance on clinical variant classification benchmark.

precision_at_cutoff.interpretation: Precision achieved for likely pathogenic and likely benign classifications on ClinVar.

mave_spearman.interpretation: Agreement with experimental multiplexed assays of variant effect (ProteinGym).

essentiality_auroc.interpretation: Performance at predicting cell essentiality for genes underpowered for LOEUF.

proteome_coverage.interpretation: Fraction of missense variants classified as either likely benign or likely pathogenic.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. AlphaMissense achieved 90% precision on the ClinVar dataset and provided predictions for all possible human missense variants (71 million), closing the variant interpretation gap.
